title: Glassy-jelly Ukrainian Salad Tart
description: A unique Ukrainian dish consisting of a glassy-jelly filling inside a katsuramuki-cut puff pastry tart. Suitable for a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: Grated carrot
ingredients.notes: Use a vegetable peeler to get thin strips

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Cut the pastry into a 9-inch (23 cm) square.
Transfer the pastry to the prepared baking sheet.
Cut the pastry into katsuramuki-cut shapes, about 1 inch (2.5 cm) wide.
In a small bowl, mix together the cottage cheese, chopped dill, chopped chives, grated carrot, granulated sugar, unflavored gelatin, lemon juice, and salt.
Spread the cheese mixture on one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch (1 cm) border.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Bake the tart for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
While the tart is baking, toast the chopped walnuts in a small skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tart and sprinkle with toasted walnuts.
Garnish with additional chopped fresh dill and chives, if desired.
